other_observers: 01/20/2008 Jacob Cooper and myself; 01/19/08 I found the bird and could not believe my eyes (no camera) 01/20/08 returned with Jacob Cooper and a camera. I posted an email on wsbn that evening 01/21/08 Ron Lambeth (info via email) 01/22/08 Larry Arnold " "
specific_location: At Horsetheif Canyon State Wildlife Area within 200 feet of duck blind # 5, which is on the south bank of the CO river 200 yards upstream of the Loma boat ramp which is on the north bank. 39 degrees 10 minutes 27.38 seconds north/108 degrees 48 minutes 15.15 seconds west according to GoogleEarth
description: A long bodied passerine bird (about 10 to 12 inches with the tail, but not more than a foot long) with a graduated tail. Bright rufous tail, back, wings,nape and head. Breast, belly and flanks dirty white to very pale yellowish; boldly streaked with dark brown. Throat brighter white and surrounded or bordered by a "collar" of broken vertical streaks. Bill dark, rather long and slightly curved at the tip. Eyes with bright yellow iris and black pupils. Some white on the sides of the face (but not a well defined pattern) on brownish background. One or maybe two very faint white wing bars. Undertail coverts appeared white and unstreaked.Behaviors: Observed feeding on the ground with some "thrashing" behavior, but not very vigorous. It kept to the willows and skulked about on the ground or in the willows at a height of 5 to 6 feet and below. Flight was undulating or oscillating.Call: Initially heard calling from across the CO river giving a loud two syllable call that I was completely unfamilar with. It was similar to a brief part of the song on the "Stokes Eastern Bird Songs" CD. It gave a loud "chuck" call as it landed low in dense willows behind me. This call matches one on the Stokes CD rather well.Plumage: only one
